units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.subtitle: Happy, Sad, Angry, Sleepy, Hungry, Thirsty
grammar_focus: Yes, I am / No, I am not.

lessons.title: How Are You?
lessons.topic: Basic emotions and How are you?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How does the sun make you feel in the video?
What makes the boy excited in the video?
What kind of day is shown in the video?

items.example: How are you? I am happy.
Are you sad? No, I am not.

steps.title: Amy and Tom's Day
steps.text: Hello, Amy! How do you feel? I am happy. How are you, Tom? I am sad. Where is my teddy bear?

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about how you feel.
examples: How are you? I am excited.
How are you? I am sleepy.
How are you? I am okay.

items.sentence: How am you? I am happy.
How are you? I is sad.

steps.audio_text: Hello, my friend! Are you feeling happy today? I am not sad because I have my favorite toy!

questions.question_text: I ___ Ben. (Grammar question)
Which word means feeling joy? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'I am happy' means you feel good.
'How are you?' 'I am ___'. Fill the blank. (Grammar review)
What is the question we ask to know someone's feelings? (Reading comprehension)

lessons.title: I Want Water
lessons.topic: Physical needs and Are you...? questions

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do we drink when we are thirsty?
questions.hint: It's clear and falls from the sky.

questions.question_text: What do you need when you feel hot on a sunny day?
questions.hint: You drink it to cool down.

questions.question_text: How do you feel after playing at the playground?

items.example: Are you hungry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am thirsty.
Do you want to eat? Yes, I do.
Are you thirsty? Yes, I will drink.

steps.title: Park Adventure
steps.text: How are you? I am hungry. Are you thirsty? Yes, I am. Let's eat a sandwich and drink some juice. Are you happy now? Yes, I am!

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about feelings.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.
Are you cold? No, I am not.

items.sentence: Are you thirsty? No, I ___ not.
Are you are happy? Yes, I am.

steps.audio_text: Hi Lucy, do you want to eat something? No, thank you. I just want a drink. I'm a bit thirsty.

questions.question_text: Are you hungry? (Grammar question)
What do you drink when thirsty? (Vocabulary question)
Tim feels thirsty. What does Tim want? (Reading comprehension question)
Fill in the blank: Are you ___? No, I am not. (Grammar question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What color is the sky on a sunny day?
questions.hint: It's the same color as calm ocean waves.

questions.question_text: What animal says 'woof woof'?
questions.hint: This pet wags its tail and loves to play fetch.

questions.question_text: What do you do when you feel sleepy?
questions.hint: You do this to rest and close your eyes.

items.example: Are you angry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am sleepy.
Are you mad? No, I am not.
It is night. I am sleepy.

steps.title: Sleepy Night
steps.text: It is night. I am sleepy. My dog is sleepy too. Are you mad? No, I am not. I am happy.

steps.explanation: Ask and answer how you feel.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you angry? No, I am not.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.

items.sentence: My face is ___. I am angry.
My face is ___. I am happy.
My face is red. I am feel angry.
My face are yellow. I am happy.

steps.audio_text: Amy, is it time for bed? Yes, I am very sleepy. Today was so much fun.

steps.prompt: Say: My face is red. I am mad.
steps.expected_text: my face is red. i am mad.

questions.question_text: What do you feel at night when you want to rest? (Vocabulary question)
My face is [color]. I am [feeling]. Fill in the blank: My face is red. I am _____. (Grammar question)
True or False: If it is night, we go to bed.
What is the opposite of sleepy? (Vocabulary question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What drink do you choose when you need to cool down?
How do you feel when you haven't had a drink for a while?
Which drink should you reach for to stay healthy?
questions.hint: It's the most natural and has no sugar.

steps.title: A Happy Day
steps.text: Hi, I am Lucy. How are you? I am happy because I am playing. Are you happy too? Yes, I am!

steps.audio_text: Hello! How are you today? I'm happy because I have a new toy to play with. Do you like toys?

steps.prompt: Say: How are you feeling now and why?
steps.expected_text: i am happy because i am playing

questions.question_text: I ___ happy today. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'happy'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'I am thirsty' means you want to eat.
How do you feel when you get a new toy?
